https://fazm.ai/blog/open-source-llm-rel.title: Open Source LLM Releases in April 2026: Every Model Worth Running
https://fazm.ai/blog/open-source-llm-rel.text:
URL Source: https://fazm.ai/blog/open-source-llm-releases-april-2026

Published Time: 2026-04-10

Markdown Content:
## Open Source LLM Releases in April 2026

April 2026 has been the busiest single month for open source LLMs in history. Meta, Alibaba, Google, and several smaller labs all shipped models in the same two-week window, giving developers more options than ever for running capable models without API dependencies. This post covers every open source LLM released in April 2026, with benchmarks, hardware requirements, and practical notes on which ones are actually worth downloading.

## The April 2026 LLM Landscape at a Glance

| Model | Organization | Date | Parameters | License | Best For | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | Llama 4 Scout | Meta | Apr 5 | 109B (17B active) MoE | Llama 4 Community | Long context (10M tokens) | | Llama 4 Maverick | Meta | Apr 5 | 400B (17B active) MoE | Llama 4 Community | Multilingual, code | | Qwen 3 | Alibaba | Apr 8 | 0.6B / 4B / 8B / 14B / 32B / 72B | Apache 2.0 | Reasoning, tool use | | Qwen 3 MoE | Alibaba | Apr 8 | 235B (22B active) | Apache 2.0 | Cost-efficient inference | | Gemma 3n | Google | Apr 9 | 2B / 4B (effective) | Gemma | On-device, multimodal | | OLMo 2 32B | Ai2 | Apr 3 | 32B | Apache 2.0 | Research, full reproducibility | | Command A | Cohere | Apr 7 | 111B (11B active) MoE | CC-BY-NC | RAG, enterprise search |

Note

Parameter counts for MoE (Mixture of Experts) models show total parameters first, then active parameters per token in parentheses. Active parameters determine your actual VRAM requirement during inference.

## Llama 4: Meta Ships Two Models at Once

Meta released Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick on April 5, 2026. Both use a mixture-of-experts architecture, which means the total parameter count is high but only a fraction of those parameters activate for any given token.

**Llama 4 Scout** is the model to watch if you care about context length. It supports 10 million tokens of context natively, which is the longest context window in any open source model as of this writing. The 17B active parameters keep inference costs reasonable, and it runs on a single H100 GPU with quantization.

**Llama 4 Maverick** targets the performance end. With 128 experts and 17B active parameters per token, it competes with GPT-4o on coding benchmarks and multilingual tasks. The tradeoff: you need significantly more VRAM to host all 400B parameters, even if only 17B activate at inference time.

| Benchmark | Llama 4 Scout | Llama 4 Maverick | GPT-4o (reference) | |---|---|---|---| | MMLU Pro | 74.3 | 80.5 | 81.2 | | HumanEval | 72.0 | 81.7 | 84.1 | | MATH-500 | 78.4 | 85.1 | 86.0 | | Context Window | 10M | 1M | 128K | | Active Params | 17B | 17B | Unknown |

Both models ship under the Llama 4 Community License, which allows commercial use but includes Meta's standard acceptable-use restrictions. If you need a fully permissive license, look at Qwen 3 or OLMo 2.

## Qwen 3: The Apache 2.0 Powerhouse

# Nvidia-supported SiFive reaches a $3.65 billion valuation with its open AI chip technology

[101 finance](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/11/nvidia-backed-sifive-hits-3-65-billion-valuation-for-open-ai-chips/)2026/04/11 14:12

By: 101 finance

## SiFive Secures $400 Million in Funding, Reaching $3.65 Billion Valuation

SiFive, established in 2015 by engineers from UC Berkeley who pioneered an open-source chip architecture, has successfully raised an impressive $400 million in an oversubscribed funding round, bringing the company’s valuation to $3.65 billion.

### Why This Investment Matters

This development is notable for several reasons. Unlike the dominant x86 and ARM processors that power most of today’s AI hardware, SiFive’s RISC-V architecture offers an open alternative. These two established CPU types are the backbone of Nvidia’s AI computing systems, making SiFive’s approach a significant departure from the norm.

### Major Backers and Investors

Nvidia participated in this funding round, joining a diverse group of venture capitalists, private equity firms, and hedge funds. The round was led by Atreides Management, founded by former Fidelity executive Gavin Baker, who also invested in Cerebras Systems’ $1 billion round. Other notable investors include Apollo Global Management, D1 Capital Partners, Point72 Turion, T. Rowe Price, Sutter Hill Ventures, and several others.

### SiFive’s Licensing Model

SiFive operates similarly to how Arm once did, by licensing its chip blueprints to companies who then adapt them for their own products, rather than manufacturing chips themselves. In contrast, Arm shifted its strategy in March by launching its first in-house AI chip, developed in partnership with Meta and adopted by clients such as OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare.

### Open and Independent Chip Design

SiFive distinguishes itself with open, non-proprietary chip designs that remain vendor-neutral and aren’t tied to specific customers. According to Pitchbook, the company’s last fundraising was in March 2022, when it secured $175 million led by Coatue Management at a pre-money valuation of $2.33 billion. That round also included Intel Capital, Qualcomm Ventures, and Aramco Ventures.

https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.text:
URL Source: https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/

Markdown Content:
Meta unveiled Muse Spark on April 8, 2026, its first AI model from the newly formed Meta Superintelligence Labs. The natively multimodal model handles text, images, audio, and tools, competing with leading systems from OpenAI and Google on benchmarks like GPQA and HealthBench.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Model Capabilities and Reasoning Modes

Muse Spark features three reasoning modes: Instant for quick responses, Thinking for chain-of-thought on harder problems, and Contemplating, which deploys multiple agents in parallel to solve complex tasks faster than sequential methods. It supports visual chain-of-thought, allowing step-by-step reasoning on images, and multi-agent orchestration for tasks like trip planning with subagents handling itineraries and activities simultaneously.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") The model was trained with reinforcement learning that penalizes excessive thinking time, enabling efficient performance on math and science problems.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Benchmark Performance and Health Focus

On HealthBench Hard, Muse Spark achieved 42.8, surpassing GPT-5.4 at 40.1 and Gemini 3.1 Pro at 20.6, with training data curated by over 1,000 physicians for health reasoning like nutrition and drug information.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[4](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") It excels in visual STEM tasks, generating interactive displays and minigames, and performs strongly in multimodal reasoning under independent evaluation.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Deployment and Infrastructure Overhaul

Apr 10, 2026 - [Technology](https://www.axios.com/technology)

# Frightening AI advances speed race to secure critical infrastructure

![Image 1](https://www.axios.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.axios.com%2FcsbomMKRj0kPnUXAWXrApyCaAI0%3D%2F52x0%2Fsmart%2F2024%2F02%2F14%2F1707954926919.jpg&w=128&q=75)

*   [Sam Sabin](https://www.axios.com/authors/ssabin)

*   email (opens in new window) 
*   sms (opens in new window) 
*   facebook (opens in new window) twitter (opens in new window) linkedin (opens in new window) bluesky (opens in new window) 

[Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com)

Add Axios as your preferred source to

see more of our stories on Google.

[Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com)

![Image 2: Illustration of a clock with binary code with the hands nearly at midnight](https://images.axios.com/stofkWNutXIUZvqv0Yvyc8STM7Y=/0x0:1920x1080/1920x1080/2026/02/13/1771014580940.jpeg?w=3840)

Illustration: Sarah Grillo/Axios

Anyone charged with securing America's critical infrastructure — water, electricity, health care, financial services and more — is officially on the clock.

**Why it matters:**AI models are getting so good at autonomously hacking and exploiting severe bugs that their own creators are holding them back out of fear they'll wreak havoc on the systems that power daily life.

*   Anthropic is limiting access to its Mythos Preview model for that reason. OpenAI is looking to do the same for a forthcoming release, a source familiar with those plans told Axios.
*   But such capabilities won't remain locked down for long.
*   Already, researchers have found readily available [open-weights models](https://aisle.com/blog/ai-cybersecurity-after-mythos-the-jagged-frontier)that are capable of exploiting many of the decades-old bugs that Mythos Preview targeted in testing. 

**Driving the news:**Anthropic said Tuesday it has no plans to release its [Mythos Preview model](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/07/anthropic-mythos-preview-cybersecurity-risks) to the general public, and other models in the Mythos line won't come up unless strict guardrails are in place and defenders have had more time to prepare.

https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models.title: ChatGPT Models Explained: Complete Comparison Guide (2026)
https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models.text:
URL Source: https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models/chatgpt-models-explained-complete-comparison-2026

Published Time: 2026-01-06T00:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
**ChatGPT's 2026 lineup is built around the GPT-5 family: GPT-5.3 Instant (the default for every tier, including free), GPT-5.4 Thinking (paid tiers, deeper reasoning), and GPT-5.4 Pro (the highest-capability option for Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Edu).** A "Legacy Models" tab preserves GPT-5.2 Thinking for 90 days after the GPT-5.4 launch. Older models - GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, o4-mini, and the original GPT-5 (Instant and Thinking) - were retired from ChatGPT on February 13, 2026 (OpenAI, 2026). ChatGPT now uses a single auto-switching router that picks Instant or Thinking based on the query. **ChatGPT Toolbox**, a Chrome extension with 18,000+ active users and a 4.5/5 Chrome Web Store rating, works with every current ChatGPT model and adds folders, full-text search, bulk export, prompt library, and prompt chaining to organize conversations across Instant, Thinking, and Pro modes.

OpenAI dramatically simplified the ChatGPT model picker in 2026. Where 2025 had a confusing mix of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.5, o1, o3, o3-pro, and o4-mini, today there are three primary choices: **Instant**, **Thinking**, and **Pro**. Each maps to a specific capability tier in the GPT-5 family. This guide walks through every current model - capabilities, tier availability, context windows, thinking-time controls, and benchmarks - then gives you a task-first decision framework so you pick the right one every time. We'll close with real-world use cases by profession and answer the 8 questions people search most.

![Image 1: ChatGPT 2026 model lineup showing GPT-5.3 Instant, GPT-5.4 Thinking, and GPT-5.4 Pro](https://media.happyhourhub.co/ChatGPT-Toolbox-Media/blog88_1.webp)
## The 2026 ChatGPT Lineup: Only GPT-5 Family

**As of February 13, 2026, every active ChatGPT model belongs to the GPT-5 family. GPT-4 series and o-series models are retired.** OpenAI merged reasoning and general-purpose intelligence into a unified design, with one router (Instant) that can auto-switch to deeper reasoning (Thinking) when a query needs it.

The full list of retired models (Feb 13, 2026):

*   **GPT-4o** - the 2024-2025 flagship multimodal model (ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, and Edu keep GPT-4o in Custom GPTs until April 3, 2026)
*   **GPT-4.1** - the specialized coding model with a 1M token context window
*   **GPT-4.1 mini** - the lightweight version of GPT-4.1
*   **OpenAI o4-mini** - the small reasoning model in the o-series lineup
*   **GPT-5 Instant** and **GPT-5 Thinking** - the original GPT-5 models (August 2025)

API access to these retired models remains unchanged for developers with existing integrations, though OpenAI has not committed to a long-term API availability window. New projects should start on the current GPT-5.3 / GPT-5.4 models.

https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gp.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gp.text:
URL Source: https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/

Markdown Content:
OpenAI retired several legacy AI models from its ChatGPT platform on February 13, 2026, including the controversial GPT-4o alongside GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, OpenAI o4-mini, and GPT-5 Instant and Thinking variants. The company stated that usage had overwhelmingly shifted to newer models like GPT-5.2[2](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source")[5](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source").

## Models Affected and Timeline

The retirement affects GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, OpenAI o4-mini, and previously announced GPT-5 variants from ChatGPT effective February 13, 2026[2](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source")[5](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source"). OpenAI provided notice in late January, following an earlier attempt to retire GPT-4o in August 2025 during the GPT-5 rollout, which was reversed due to user backlash[1](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source")[4](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source"). ChatGPT Enterprise workspaces retain access to GPT-5 models until February 19, 2026, while Business, Enterprise, and Edu customers can use GPT-4o in Custom GPTs until March 31 or April 3, 2026[2](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source"). Existing conversations and projects using retired models default to GPT-5.2 after the date[2](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source").

## User Usage and Backlash

OpenAI reported that only 0.1% of users selected GPT-4o daily prior to retirement, equating to roughly 800,000 people among 800 million weekly active users[5](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source")[7](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source"). Despite low overall usage, some users expressed strong attachment, describing GPT-4o as warmer and more conversational, with complaints about losing its style in newer models[1](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source")[4](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source"). The retirement timing on the eve of Valentine's Day drew criticism from users who had formed emotional bonds with the model[3](https://mlq.ai/news/openai-phases-out-gpt-4o-and-other-legacy-models-from-chatgpt/# "Click to view source").

# Google Just Put NotebookLM Inside Gemini: Here’s What You Can Do Now

3:00 pm April 11, 2026 By [Julian Horsey](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/author/jhorsey/)

![Image 1: B2B sales prospecting setup using NotebookLM sources to draft tailored outreach messages inside Gemini chat.](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/notebooklm-gemini-unified-interface.webp)

Google’s integration of **NotebookLM** with the **Gemini AI platform** introduces a structured way to handle information and streamline tasks. As explained by Universe of AI, one standout feature is **persistent memory**, which allows the system to remember details from previous interactions. This is particularly useful for long-term projects, as it reduces repetitive data entry and helps maintain continuity in workflows.

Explore how this update enhances **workflow organization** with features such as task categorization through folders and the ability to consolidate conversations into notebooks. Learn how customizable AI instructions can assist with tasks like summarizing complex research or creating detailed reports. Gain insight into **AI Studio**, which offers tools for organizing knowledge, including mind mapping, flashcard creation and slide deck development.

## Key Features of the NotebookLM & Gemini Integration

**TL;DR Key Takeaways :**
